commands.description: Build and freeze deterministic split manifests without training.
Run only the Gate 2-approved two-condition pilot.
Independently verify the frozen pilot evidence before Gate 3.

forbidden_scope: raw data or checkpoints in public export

experiment_contract.authorization_scope: pilot only; no full run and no public claim

builder_verifier.builder: Implements only the approved split, model, configuration, and pilot; freezes code, data, schema, split, configuration, environment, and model-state hashes; does not open test metrics.
builder_verifier.verifier: Independently checks hashes, row counts, class coverage, partition equality and disjointness, train-only normalization, feature removal, deterministic replay, metrics, runtime, VRAM, artifact hashes, and privacy boundaries without editing Builder outputs.

claim_limits: The pilot can establish execution integrity only; it cannot support a scientific conclusion.
The final walkthrough is one bounded audit, not a benchmark, novelty claim, SOTA claim, universal scientific conclusion, GPU performance claim, or proof of native compatibility across agents.
Re-execution is scoped to the recorded environment and does not promise bitwise equality across PyTorch releases, platforms, or hardware.

dataset.area4_policy: coverage failure only; class 4 has zero complementary training support, so no Area 4 model or macro-F1 comparison is allowed

environment.hardware_class: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU with 8188 MiB VRAM
environment.supply_chain_limit: Existing local environment; package installation provenance was not independently verified. No new package installation is authorized.

features.continuous_columns: columns 0 through 9; standardized with that condition's training partition only
features.limitation: Soil_Type features may remain geographic proxies, so this is not proof of complete spatial generalization.
features.removed_columns: target column 54 and Wilderness_Area columns 10 through 13

future_full_requires_gate3.test_open_policy: Open each frozen test evaluator only after all approved full training runs finish; no result-driven retries or configuration changes.

metrics_and_claim_rules.anomaly_pause: absolute mean delta at least 0.20 or any seed absolute delta at least 0.30 pauses Gate 4 for leakage and evaluator audit
metrics_and_claim_rules.guardrails: accuracy, per-class precision recall and F1, confusion matrix, prediction distribution, class coverage, validation metrics, runtime, peak VRAM, failures, and NaN counts
metrics_and_claim_rules.primary_effect: For each area, delta equals macro-F1 on that area's present labels under matched random minus whole-area holdout.
metrics_and_claim_rules.qualified_per_area: a finite audited effect that does not meet every supported threshold
metrics_and_claim_rules.supported_per_area: mean delta at least 0.05, all three seed deltas positive, bootstrap lower bound above 0.02, and no Critical verifier finding
metrics_and_claim_rules.uncertainty: mean and standard deviation across three seeds plus a fixed-seed stratified confusion-matrix bootstrap interval; this interval is descriptive and not a population-generalization guarantee
metrics_and_claim_rules.unsupported_or_reversed: bootstrap upper bound below 0.05 refutes the preregistered five-point effect for that area; an upper bound below zero records reversal

success_checks: Dataset, schema, counts, hashes, split equality, disjointness, and feature removal all match the frozen contract.
Training and validation losses are finite, and at least one later training epoch is lower than epoch one.
The Verifier independently replays the first 20 optimizer steps for both conditions with loss tolerance 1e-7 and matching canonical model-state tensor hashes.
CUDA is available on the approved GPU class, peak allocated VRAM is at most 7.2 GiB, and the projected full block fits its separate ceiling.
No integrity, privacy, security, budget, or deterministic-algorithm finding remains open.

privacy.never_record_or_export: raw data, full row indices, checkpoints, per-row predictions or logits, raw stdout or stderr, tracebacks, caches, package-manager logs, environment variables, usernames, hostnames, GPU UUIDs or serials, process lists, absolute paths, account data, credentials, tokens, or API information
privacy.public_artifacts: reviewed minimal experiment code, frozen config, dependency lock, split rules and aggregate counts, aggregate metric summary, attribution, and SHA-256 hashes

experiment_contract.research_question: For UCI Covertype, how much does measured multiclass performance change when a whole wilderness area is held out instead of using a random test split with exactly matched per-class counts?

invariants: Each condition has disjoint train, validation, and test partitions whose union is all rows.
The two conditions have exactly equal train, validation, and test counts for every label.
Split manifests and aggregate counts are frozen and hashed before any CUDA training call.
Full row ID or index lists remain local and are never included in public export.
split.random_test_algorithm: Within each label, sort all rows by SHA256('test|20260715|' + row_id) and take exactly the Area 3 test count without replacement.
split.validation_algorithm: Within each condition and label, sort the post-test pool by SHA256('val|20260716|' + row_id) and take floor(0.10 * class_count).

stop_rules: Stop on any dataset, schema, count, hash, split, code, config, evaluator, or environment drift.

Stop on target or area leakage, partition overlap or omission, unequal paired counts, test access before Gate 3, NaN or Inf, OOM, deterministic-algorithm failure, privacy or security finding, or budget ceiling.
Do not fall back to CPU, reduce batch size, retry automatically, change seeds, tune on test, or expand network and storage scope.
Any semantic contract change invalidates Gate 2 approval and requires a new human decision.

training.determinism: same initialization per paired condition; torch deterministic algorithms enabled; cuDNN benchmark disabled; fixed generator; num_workers zero
training.model: float32 MLP 50-128-64-7 with ReLU after each hidden layer

public_summary: A bounded Covertype demonstration found higher matched-random than area-holdout macro-F1 in all nine paired comparisons; the claim remains limited to the recorded dataset, model, seeds, and split protocol.

compute.accelerator: one NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU
compute.cpu: local CPU, not identified or published
